disputed.evidence: "Students will work in groups to prepare sesame cauliflower and fried rice." Materials: "Cutting boards / Knives ... Cauliflower ... Carrots ... Onion ... Broccoli ... Scallions". Prep: "Pre-cook rice" (only the rice is pre-done; vegetables are not pre-cut).
disputed.reason: Students prepare the dishes; cutting of cauliflower/carrots/onion is entailed and knives are set at student tables; no pre-cut prep step for the veg.

disputed.evidence: Materials list includes "Baking sheet / Silpat (or foil) / Oven" for the sesame cauliflower, but the Agenda only says students "prepare sesame cauliflower and fried rice" with no roasting/oven step and no statement of who operates the oven.
disputed.reason: Oven/baking-sheet are tools on the materials list; no Agenda step shows students roasting, and oven heat-work is typically teacher-operated.

disputed.evidence: Prep work/setup (teacher): "Pre-cook rice ... Chop onions/Mince garlic for beans". Engaging activity (students): "Students will work together to prepare the different components of burrito bowls: Go: Cilantro Lime Rice / Grow: Black beans / Glow: Tomato Corn Salsa" — no explicit student cutting step.
disputed.reason: The only explicit chopping ("Chop onions/Mince garlic") is teacher prep; tomato-cutting for the salsa is not stated as a student step.

disputed.evidence: Prep (teacher): "Pre-cook rice". Materials: "Cook tops / Pans". Students "prepare the different components" but no Agenda step shows students boiling/simmering the beans or rice.
disputed.reason: Rice is pre-cooked by teacher; no explicit student boiling/simmering step for the beans is shown in the Agenda.

disputed.evidence: "students collect ingredients in a bowl ... Put some dressing in each bowl and have students pass around the table to mix" / Option 2: "Combine into one large bowl and mix in chickpeas. Add dressing to taste and mix together." The verbatim action is mixing/shaking, not layered assembly.
disputed.reason: Students mix a salad (verbatim "mix"/"shake"); this is the mixing skill, not a distinct multi-component dish assembly.

disputed.evidence: "have students pass around the table to mix" / "students get to do the mixing" / "Add dressing to taste and mix together" / dressing: "students pass around the table and shake to make the dressing."
disputed.reason: Students explicitly do the mixing/shaking of the salad and dressing — Agenda-explicit student action.

disputed.evidence: "Cucumber table: cook the Hoppin' John" with "Choose one table leader to assign jobs to the other students." Materials: "Stove / Large pan". Anticipated problems: "Be careful to not overcook the rice- it becomes mushy." Prep par-cooks peas ("Pre-soak/par-cook the black eyed peas") but final cooking is student-done.
disputed.reason: Students cook the Hoppin' John (rice + black-eyed peas) on the stove in a pan = simmering; the cook step is a student rotation, not teacher-prep.

disputed.evidence: "3rd grade: make cornbread batter." "4th grade: ... taste cornbread" (pre-baked). Materials include "Oven / Baking pan" but no Agenda step shows students putting cornbread in the oven or baking it.
disputed.reason: Students make batter (a mixing step); the actual baking in the oven is not shown as a student action (4th grade tastes pre-made cornbread).

disputed.evidence: Engaging activity, Toppings Table(s): "4. Measure corn" — listed among the student prep/cook steps (alongside "Grate carrots", "Chop broccoli", etc.).
disputed.reason: "Measure corn" is a verbatim student step in the Agenda's hands-on cooking block.

title: Healthy Snacks (Energy Bites, Popcorn, Cracker Stack)

disputed.evidence: Energy bites: "Pre-made mixture of sunbutter, honey, oats, vanilla, cinnamon" (dough pre-made; students only "roll into balls"). Popcorn: "Add a pinch of seasonings and shake to mix." No bowl-stirring student step.
disputed.reason: The dough is pre-made; the only "mix" is shaking popcorn with seasoning, which is better captured by the agreed Seasoning & spice blending.

disputed.evidence: Closing: "Vote for favorite snacks" and reflection "How would you describe the taste of each of the snacks? What are some of your favorite flavors and textures?" Opening references a "tasting word wall."
disputed.reason: A favorite-snack preference vote plus casual "describe the taste" reflection — not a structured sensory comparison, rubric, or blind test.

disputed.evidence: Energy bites: "Students roll into balls using tablespoon" and "Roll balls in coconut flakes."
disputed.reason: Students explicitly roll the energy bites into balls and roll them in coconut — Agenda-explicit student rolling action.

disputed.evidence: "Teacher collects all dumplings from each table. Place in the dumplings in the pans with a bit of oil ... Steam for 3 minutes with the lid on. Teacher has two pans going." (The Culinary-Skills header lists "steam" but the Agenda action is teacher-operated.)
disputed.reason: Steaming is explicitly teacher-operated ("Teacher collects ... Teacher has two pans going"); students fill/seal dumplings, not steam them.

disputed.evidence: "Each table has a set of task cards: Team Captains help distribute the task cards ... Then each student gets a task card and complete their task, putting their ingredients in the bowl."
disputed.reason: Students each follow a task card (structured recipe step) to complete their portion of the dish — Agenda-explicit student step.

disputed.evidence: Opening: "Review Kitchen Pledge" (also in Vocabulary and Prep: "Kitchen pledge"). Opening rules: "Be Safe - listen for instructions, use tools safely." Plus a dedicated "Wash Hands (5 minutes)" segment.
disputed.reason: The Kitchen Pledge is reviewed/taught in the opening (the standard explicitly allows a taught Kitchen Pledge); low confidence because it is a brief 'review', not a full safety segment. FLAG for adjudicator.

title: September Sixth Grade Summer Plant Hunt

disputed.evidence: Closing Circle: "Introduce students to the guidelines for our tasting ... we can use our other senses: smell, touch, and sight ... What do you notice about the taste? Give me a thumbs-up if it's sweet. Give me a thumbs-up if it's sour." This is a garden plant-hunt/watering lesson with no cooking block.
disputed.reason: A single garden-produce tasting with a sweet/sour thumbs-check and senses framing, not a structured sensory comparison/rubric; and there is no hands-on cooking block at all (cooking_skills should be empty). FLAG: borderline on the sensory-vocabulary point.

disputed.evidence: "Students will work in groups to prepare chicha morada and arroz chaufa (fried rice) or locro de zapallo y papas." Materials: "Cutting boards / Knives" (set up at student tables). Task card: "Arroz Chaufa (no meat plus any available veggies)". Prep (teacher) pre-cooks rice / parboils potatoes / roasts squash, but veg-cutting for the fried rice is not pre-done.
disputed.reason: Students prepare arroz chaufa with veggies (entails cutting); knives/cutting boards are set at student tables and the veg is not pre-cut in prep. (Same template as 'Food from the Eastern Hemisphere'.)

title: Foods From Around the World: China

disputed.evidence: Materials: "Plastic bag and rolling pin for smashing cucumbers". Agenda: "Model for students how to smash the cucumber. The goal is to release the juices ... You can have students give themselves 10 seconds to smash or sing a song while they smash, then pass to a neighbor."
disputed.reason: Students crush/smash the cucumbers with a rolling pin (a crushing action; nearest skill is Mashing). FLAG: 'smash to release juice' is arguably distinct from true mashing.

disputed.evidence: Agenda combine step: "Have students who are making the dressing mix it in a jar or cup, then add it once the cucumber is done draining." The dish is cucumber + herbs + dressing.
disputed.reason: Combining drained cucumber with dressing is a small mix-in step, better captured by Mixing & stirring / Creating sauces & dressings than a distinct dish-assembly skill. FLAG: borderline.

disputed.evidence: Each tray includes a "Recipe copy"; "Take-home copies of smashed cucumber salad recipe". Anticipated problems: "Assign one student to be the table leader ... and ensure the recipe is followed."
disputed.reason: Students follow a recipe copy at each table; the table leader's role is explicitly to "ensure the recipe is followed."

disputed.evidence: Materials: "Jar or cup for mixing dressing" and "Stirring spoon". Agenda: "Have students who are making the dressing mix it in a jar or cup."
disputed.reason: Students explicitly mix the dressing and a stirring spoon is provided; low confidence because the mixing mostly overlaps the agreed Creating sauces & dressings. FLAG: overlap.

disputed.evidence: Closing ritual: "Finish by tasting what students have made together. Have students share tasting words about the tasting."
disputed.reason: "Share tasting words" is a tasting-vocabulary activity (a taught sensory skill), not merely a like/dislike preference vote.

disputed.evidence: Materials: "Garlic press or grater" (a tool option). No Agenda step instructs students to grate; garlic is for the dressing and can be pressed.
disputed.reason: Grater is a one-of-two tool option on the materials list with no student grating step in the Agenda.
